name: minor-followups-reuse-related-task-id
tags: task id, commit, gc, minor change, follow-up, reuse task, no new task, workflow preference
body:
For small follow-up changes to something just built (a logging tweak, a one-line fix, a tiny adjustment discovered right after closing a task), reuse the original/related task's id in the commit rather than creating a new task.

Why: user explicitly said so when asked whether to open a new task for a single added log line right after task:b9dded3e had already closed — creating a fresh task for something this small is unnecessary overhead.

How to apply: only spin up a new task when the follow-up is substantial enough to need its own Resolution/tracking (e.g. multi-step, needs grooming, spans multiple files). For a true one-liner or quick fix tied to recently-closed work, just reference task:<original-id> in the /gc commit even though that task is already done — the commit-gate only checks a task:<id> is present, not that it's open.
